app.name: SiriusPad
app.tagline: Scratchpad for developers. Fast, dark, and local.

sidebar.workspaceHint: Create more workspaces with the + button.

note.addTagHelp: Add a tag and press Enter to confirm.
note.deleteDirtyDescription: This note has unsaved changes. They will be saved before moving it to the trash.
note.closeDirtyDescription: Do you want to save before closing it?
note.closeWindowDescription: The active note still has unsaved changes.
note.noActiveDescription: Create a note from the sidebar or use Ctrl+N to start writing.
note.checklistHint: A local note checklist to track next steps without leaving your flow.
note.checklistEmpty: No items yet. Add quick tasks to track fixes, tests, or follow-ups.
note.calloutsHint: Insert Markdown callouts without memorizing the syntax. They turn into cards in preview.
note.calloutNoteHint: Use it for context, summaries, observations, or quick docs.
note.calloutTipHint: Great for shortcuts, helpful commands, and practical reminders.
note.calloutWarningHint: Use it when something needs care before you run or change it.
note.calloutsFooter: Supported syntax: `> [!NOTE]`, `> [!TIP]`, `> [!WARNING]`, `> [!BUG]` and similar. The editor stays in Markdown; the visual card appears in preview.
note.calloutTemplateNoteBody: Summarize an important note detail here.
note.calloutTemplateTipBody: Keep a shortcut, command, or useful insight here.
note.calloutTemplateWarningBody: Describe something that needs extra care before you continue.
note.calloutTemplateDangerBody: Record impact, suspicion, and the next step.

workspace.name: Workspace name
workspace.deleteConfirm: Delete workspace "{{name}}" and move its notes to "{{fallback}}"?

gist.publicConfirm: Choose how you want to create this Gist.
gist.tokenRequired: A GitHub token is required to export a Gist.

commands.copyAsCode: Copy note as fenced code block

editor.title: Editor
editor.description: Font, wrapping, autosave, and editor ergonomics.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Interface zoom, dark-only theme, and the font used across the UI.

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Local shortcuts active inside the app. The main command palette opens with Ctrl+K.

variables.title: Global Variables
variables.description: Substituted in snippets, copies, and exports.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: GitHub Gist export uses a token with the gist scope.

about.title: Project and security
about.description: Public SiriusPad project info, usage notices, useful links, and the right channels for support and vulnerabilities.

fields.themeLocked: SiriusPad is dark-only. Use this section to adjust interface scale and typography.
fields.uiZoomHint: Scales the whole app, similar to browser zoom.

about.licenseHint: Usage, copying, and distribution under MIT.
about.projectHint: Code, issues, changelog, and releases live on the SiriusPad GitHub repository.
about.privacyBody: Notes stay on your disk. SiriusPad does not require an account for the main flow, and external services are only used when you trigger something like Gist export or update checks.
about.securityBody: Security flaws should not go to a public issue. Use the security policy and, when needed, open a private advisory so fixes can happen responsibly.
about.commandsBody: The embedded terminal and snippet runner execute commands on your machine. Review scripts, snippets, and commands before running them, especially from third parties.
about.integrationsBody: The GitHub token for Gist is stored locally. Use only the scope you need and remove the token when you no longer need it.

searchPanel.placeholder: Type a command or search notes...
searchPanel.commandHelp: Quick actions, note navigation, and app controls in one place.

updater.description: A new version is ready to install.
updater.releaseNotesEmpty: No detailed notes were published for this release.
updater.actionCopy: The app downloads the package, prepares installation, and restarts when you confirm in the final step.
updater.backgroundDownload: You can keep using the app while the download finishes in the background.
updater.restartNotice: The download has finished. Restart SiriusPad now to install the new version.

terminal.title: Terminal
terminal.subtitle: cmd.exe on Windows and bash on Linux/macOS, always pointed at the active note folder.
terminal.sessionReadyShort: {{shell}} ready. Use it like a normal terminal and Ctrl+C to interrupt processes.
terminal.ready: {{shell}} ready. Type a command and press Enter.
terminal.noteDirectoryReady: Terminal now points to the note folder: {{cwd}}
terminal.processRunning: There is already a process running in the terminal.
terminal.blockMissingLanguage: This block has no language set. Mark it as js, ts, py, or bash to run it correctly.
terminal.blockUnsupported: This block is marked as {{language}}, which is not executable here. It was sent to the prompt so you can adjust it.
terminal.runningBlock: Running {{language}} block with the correct interpreter in the note folder.
terminal.snippetFinished: Execution finished in {{ms}} ms with exit code {{code}}.
terminal.emptyHint: Type a {{shell}} command below or use "Run note" to execute the note content with the correct interpreter.
terminal.runHint: Enter runs. Ctrl+C cancels the current process.
terminal.cwdHint: Use cd to move around, or jump back to the note folder with one click.
terminal.outputHint: Run the whole note with Ctrl+Enter or use the ▶ button on blocks that declare a language.
terminal.unsupportedHint: Output appears here when the note language supports execution.

welcome.title: Welcome to SiriusPad
welcome.description: SiriusPad is built to keep bugs, ideas, snippets, commands, and technical notes nearby without slowing your flow.

notes.title: Fast notes, still organized
notes.description: Each note combines title, Markdown, checklist, tags, priority, color, and workspace. Use the checklist for quick tasks and callouts to highlight context in preview.

tags.title: Read context at a glance
tags.description: Colored tags and priority make it easy to spot whether something is a bug, urgent item, idea, or already done.

terminal.title: A real embedded terminal
terminal.description: Open it with Ctrl+`. SiriusPad uses the real platform shell and starts in the active note folder. Terminal commands go to the shell; js, ts, py, and bash blocks run with the right interpreter.

command.title: Command palette, made clear
command.description: Ctrl+K opens the app command center. Type an action, note name, or workspace; use arrow keys to navigate and Enter to run. Think of it as the fastest way to find anything without breaking flow.

project.title: Public project, responsible usage
project.description: SiriusPad is maintained by SiriusX as a public project. It is local-first, MIT licensed, and explicit when an action touches the terminal, token, disk, or an external integration.

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts worth memorizing
shortcuts.description: These are the ones that speed up everyday use the most. You do not need all of them now, but they stay visible in the UI.

practice.title: Test it in practice
practice.description: After closing the tutorial, follow this short script to validate the app's main promises in real use.

ready.title: Ready to go
ready.description: If you forget something, you can reopen the tutorial from settings. The goal is to be quick to start and comfortable to keep using.

visuals.notesBody: Fix updater flow and review window shortcuts
visuals.welcomeBadge: A dark, local, no-nonsense pad for people who live between code, terminal, and checklists.
visuals.welcomePoint1: Markdown for writing fast without losing structure.
visuals.welcomePoint2: Workspaces to separate context without clutter.
visuals.welcomePoint3: Terminal, history, and shortcuts wired into the flow.
visuals.welcomePoint4: A public project maintained by SiriusX with a practical developer-first focus.
visuals.notesCalloutBody: Use > [!TIP] and > [!WARNING] blocks to highlight important information.
visuals.practiceStep1Body: Use Ctrl+N, give the note a title, and pick a color from the right panel. That color should appear in the header, tab, and note list.
visuals.practiceStep2Body: In the right panel, create at least 2 checklist items and mark 1 as done. The counter should follow along without cluttering your writing area.
visuals.practiceStep3Body: Still in the right panel, use one of the quick callout blocks, switch to preview or split mode, and confirm it becomes a highlighted card with a side accent.
visuals.practiceStep4Body: Open it with Ctrl+`, run a simple command like `pwd`, `ls`, or `dir`, and confirm it starts in the active note folder.
visuals.practiceFooter: If this script works, you have already validated note color, checklist, callouts, preview, and terminal without relying on vague promises.
visuals.projectPoint1: Developed by SiriusX and shipped as a public GitHub project.
visuals.projectPoint2: MIT license for use, study, modification, and distribution.
visuals.projectPoint3: Notes stay local; external integrations are optional.
visuals.projectPoint4: Terminal and snippets run on your machine, so review before executing.
visuals.readyBadge: Everything stays local and the app opens ready to continue.
visuals.readyHint: Start with Ctrl+N, pick a color from the right panel, add a checklist item, insert a callout with one click, and open the terminal with Ctrl+` to test the full flow.
visuals.readyPoint1: Settings now show version, license, project links, and the security policy.
visuals.readyPoint2: You can reopen this tutorial anytime from settings.
visuals.readyPoint3: Use issues for bugs and a private advisory for vulnerabilities.

rightPanel.noteToolsHint: Keep writing in the center and use this panel for color, checklist, and quick blocks.
rightPanel.noteToolsEmpty: Open a note to customize color, checklist, and quick blocks.
rightPanel.checklistHint: A simple editable side checklist that does not pollute the editor.
rightPanel.calloutsHint: Insert quick blocks and see the result in preview without pulling focus away from the note.
